description: Interdisciplinary study linking sleep duration and quality to cognitive performance, workplace productivity, and macroeconomic outcomes. Bridges neuroscience (memory consolidation, prefrontal function), occupational health (accident risk, absenteeism), and economics (GDP impact of insufficient sleep). Unique because individual-level sleep data exists at massive scale (wearables, time-use surveys) but cross-domain synthesis is rare.
population: Working-age adults (18-65), population surveys, wearable device users, shift workers
